mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals impending corporate earnings declines and recession risk, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month rolling window
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving higher corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series models, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth during a monetary tightening cycle indicates resilient economic activity that offsets higher borrowing costs, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations remain intact.
failure_condition: The signal direction flips to negative during tightening periods, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with low policy rates compresses real yields and erodes purchasing power, prompting immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of triggered instances, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and consumer spending power, mechanically supporting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or payroll growth shows no correlation with 20-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth during an early-cycle expansion phase signals strong top-line revenue growth for S&P 500 constituents, driving sustained positive forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power or turns negative when applied to late-cycle or recessionary periods, or 60-day returns are flat during GDP accelerations.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal deteriorating manufacturing demand and potential layoffs, triggering risk aversion and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the production decline signal fails to predict equity drawdowns in out-of-sample tests.
